loss_of_spouse.headline: California: Estate & Survivor Rules
bullets: Community property state — jointly held assets transfer to the surviving spouse automatically, often without probate
Simplified probate (Petition to Determine Succession to Property) available for estates under $184,500 (2024 threshold)
No California estate tax; federal estate tax applies only above $13.61M
Order certified death certificates from the county recorder's office — budget for 10–15 copies
Spousal Property Petition allows quick court confirmation of community property without full probate administration

divorce.headline: California: Divorce Rules
bullets: Mandatory 6-month waiting period from the date the respondent is served — no exceptions
Community property state — marital assets and debts are presumed to be divided 50/50 by default
Spousal support duration is typically half the length of the marriage for marriages under 10 years
California Family Law Facilitator offices (in every county) provide free help with court paperwork
File in Superior Court in the county where either spouse has lived for at least 3 months

job_loss.headline: California: Job Loss Benefits
bullets: File for EDD unemployment at edd.ca.gov within the first week — benefits up to $450/wk for up to 26 weeks
California SDI (State Disability Insurance) provides up to 52 weeks of benefits if illness or injury prevents work
CA WARN Act requires 60-day advance notice for mass layoffs at companies with 75+ workers
California prohibits non-compete agreements — you are legally free to work for a competitor immediately
File EDD UI claim immediately — California does not back-pay for weeks prior to your filing date

relocation.headline: California: Relocation Requirements
bullets: Update your California driver's license within 10 days of establishing new residency in the state
Register your vehicle with the DMV within 20 days of bringing it into California
California requires a smog check for most vehicles at initial registration
Register to vote at your new address — California offers same-day voter registration at the polls
AB 60 licenses are available regardless of immigration status

disability.headline: California: Disability Benefits
bullets: California SDI pays 60–70% of wages (up to approximately $1,620/wk in 2024) for up to 52 weeks
File your SDI claim with EDD within 49 days of disability onset or you may lose benefits
California has no waiting period for SDI — benefits begin after 7 days (vs. federal SSDI's 5-month wait)
IHSS (In-Home Supportive Services) provides paid in-home care for eligible Medi-Cal recipients
California's SDI program can bridge the gap during the 5-month federal SSDI waiting period

retirement.headline: California: Retirement Taxes & Rules
bullets: California taxes Social Security income — there is no state-level SS exemption
State income tax rates reach up to 13.3%, making pre-retirement Roth conversions worth considering
CalPERS and CalSTRS pension income is subject to California income tax
No California estate tax; however CA has unique capital gains treatment on inherited property
Medicare and Social Security enrollment follow federal timelines; CalPERS may offer retiree health coverage

loss_of_spouse.headline: Texas: Estate & Survivor Rules
bullets: Community property state — assets owned jointly transfer to the surviving spouse automatically
Muniment of Title is a streamlined Texas court process to transfer real estate without full probate administration
Affidavit of Heirship can establish real property title without a court proceeding for qualifying estates
No Texas state estate tax or state income tax
Texas allows Independent Administration — a faster, less court-supervised probate option if specified in the will

divorce.headline: Texas: Divorce Rules
bullets: 60-day waiting period from the date of filing before a divorce can be finalized (one of the shortest in the US)
Community property state — marital property is presumed to be split equally absent compelling reasons
Texas allows fault-based divorce (cruelty, adultery, abandonment) which can affect property division
Spousal maintenance is limited — typically requires 10+ years of marriage or documented family violence
File in the district court of the county where either spouse has lived for 90+ days

job_loss.headline: Texas: Job Loss Benefits
bullets: File for TWC unemployment at twc.texas.gov within 2 weeks of job loss — benefits up to $563/wk for 26 weeks
Texas is a strict at-will employment state — no state-specific termination protections beyond federal law
Federal WARN Act requires 60 days' notice for mass layoffs at companies with 100+ employees
No Texas income tax means unemployment benefits are only subject to federal income tax
Texas Workforce Commission (TWC) offers free skills assessments, job matching, and training at any WorkInTexas.com office

relocation.headline: Texas: Relocation Requirements
bullets: Obtain a Texas driver's license within 90 days of establishing residency — bring current license, proof of residence, and SS card
Register your vehicle with the county Tax Assessor-Collector within 30 days
Annual vehicle safety inspection required — most inspection stations also process registration
Apply for homestead exemption by April 30 for significant property tax savings on your primary residence
Register to vote at least 30 days before an election — Texas does not have same-day voter registration

disability.headline: Texas: Disability Benefits
bullets: Texas has no state-run short-term disability insurance program — SSDI is the primary federal option
File for SSDI as soon as disability begins — the 5-month waiting period starts from your disability onset date
HHSC (Health and Human Services Commission) manages STAR+PLUS Medicaid for long-term care services
DARS (Dept. of Assistive and Rehabilitative Services) offers free vocational rehabilitation and job training
No state income tax means SSDI benefits are only subject to federal income tax rules

retirement.headline: Texas: Retirement Taxes & Rules
bullets: No Texas state income tax — Social Security, pensions, 401(k), and IRA withdrawals are completely state-tax-free
TRS (Teacher Retirement System of Texas) and ERS (Employees Retirement System) are the major state pension systems
Property taxes are among the highest in the country — the over-65 homestead exemption is critical to claim promptly
No Texas estate or inheritance tax
Medicare enrollment follows federal timelines; Texas has no state supplemental Medicare program

loss_of_spouse.headline: Florida: Estate & Survivor Rules
bullets: Common law property state — assets held solely in your spouse's name will go through probate
Summary Administration available for estates worth less than $75,000 or where death occurred more than 2 years ago
Florida homestead law strongly protects the surviving spouse — right to life estate or undivided 50% interest
No Florida estate tax or state income tax
File for probate in the Circuit Court of the county where the decedent lived

divorce.headline: Florida: Divorce Rules
bullets: No mandatory waiting period — divorce can be finalized as soon as the court schedules a final hearing
Florida is a pure no-fault state — irretrievable breakdown of the marriage is the only required ground
Equitable distribution: courts divide marital assets fairly, not necessarily 50/50
Required parenting course (4 hours) for all cases involving minor children
Permanent alimony was abolished in 2023 — durational alimony is now capped based on marriage length

job_loss.headline: Florida: Job Loss Benefits
bullets: File with DEO (Dept. of Economic Opportunity) at connect.myflorida.com — benefits range $32–$275/wk (some of the lowest in the US)
Maximum benefit duration is 12–23 weeks depending on the statewide unemployment rate
Apply immediately — Florida does not back-pay for weeks you didn't file a weekly certification
No Florida income tax means unemployment benefits are only taxed at the federal level
Florida is an at-will state with no additional termination protections beyond federal law

relocation.headline: Florida: Relocation Requirements
bullets: Obtain a Florida driver's license within 30 days of establishing residency
Register your vehicle within 10 days of becoming a Florida resident
No statewide vehicle inspection required (emissions testing applies in some South Florida counties)
File for homestead exemption by March 1 of the year following your move for property tax savings
No state income tax — Florida is a common destination for tax-motivated relocation

disability.headline: Florida: Disability Benefits
bullets: Florida has no state short-term disability insurance program — federal SSDI is the primary resource
SSDI 5-month federal waiting period applies; file immediately after disability onset
Florida Division of Vocational Rehabilitation (VR) provides free job training, placement, and adaptive equipment
Medicaid through AHCA is available for qualifying low-income individuals with disabilities
Workers' compensation covers on-the-job injuries — report to your employer within 30 days to preserve your claim

retirement.headline: Florida: Retirement Taxes & Rules
bullets: No Florida state income tax — Social Security, pensions, 401(k), and IRA distributions are completely state-tax-free
Florida Retirement System (FRS) covers state employees and most local government employees
No Florida estate or inheritance tax
Homestead exemption ($50,000) reduces property taxes on your primary residence — file promptly
Medigap plans are widely available and competitively priced in Florida

loss_of_spouse.headline: New York: Estate & Survivor Rules
bullets: Probate is handled through the Surrogate's Court in the county where the decedent lived
Voluntary Administration available for estates under $50,000 (simplified and faster process)
New York imposes a state estate tax on estates over $6.94M (2024) at rates up to 16%
Surviving spouse is entitled to an 'elective share' — the right to claim at least 1/3 of the net estate
Order death certificates from NYC DOHMH (city residents) or county clerk (upstate) — get 10–15 certified copies

divorce.headline: New York: Divorce Rules
bullets: No-fault divorce available — irretrievable breakdown of the marriage for 6+ months is sufficient
Equitable distribution of marital property — courts weigh many factors, not simply 50/50
Spousal maintenance guidelines are formula-based, making outcomes more predictable
File in Supreme Court (the trial-level court in NY despite the name) in the county of residence
A parenting plan and child support order must be in place before a divorce with minor children is granted

job_loss.headline: New York: Job Loss Benefits
bullets: File with NYSDOL at labor.ny.gov within the first week — benefits up to $504/wk for up to 26 weeks
NY WARN Act requires 90 days' notice for layoffs (stricter than the federal 60-day requirement)
If your employer violated NY WARN, you may be owed up to 90 days of back pay and benefits
NYC residents: Workforce1 Career Centers offer free job placement, training, and resume services
Apply within the first week — New York does not back-pay more than one week prior to your filing date

relocation.headline: New York: Relocation Requirements
bullets: Obtain a New York driver's license within 30 days of establishing residency
Register your vehicle with the DMV within 30 days
STAR program: register for school tax relief on your primary residence — Basic STAR is available to most homeowners
NYC residents: update Con Edison/National Grid, MTA transit accounts, and borough-specific services
Enhanced STAR is available for homeowners 65+ with qualifying income — file by the county deadline

disability.headline: New York: Disability Benefits
bullets: New York has mandatory short-term disability (DBL) insurance — benefits up to $170/wk for up to 26 weeks
File your DBL claim through your employer's carrier within 30 days of disability onset
New York Paid Family Leave (PFL) provides up to 67% of your AWW for qualifying family situations
SSDI federal 5-month waiting period still applies for long-term disability
NY ACCES-VR (Adult Career and Continuing Education Services) provides vocational rehabilitation support

retirement.headline: New York: Retirement Taxes & Rules
bullets: Social Security income is fully exempt from New York state income tax
Pensions from NY state/local government and the federal government are fully exempt from NY tax
Private pensions and retirement account distributions: up to $20,000/year exempt for those 59½+
NYC residents pay an additional city income tax on non-exempt retirement income
NYSLRS and TRS (Teacher Retirement System) are the major New York public pension systems

loss_of_spouse.headline: Pennsylvania: Estate & Survivor Rules
bullets: File with the Register of Wills in the county where the decedent lived
Small Estate process available for gross estates under $50,000 (simpler than full probate)
Pennsylvania Inheritance Tax: 0% for surviving spouse — no tax on any assets passing to you
Children of the decedent pay 4.5% inheritance tax; other beneficiaries pay 12–15%
No Pennsylvania estate tax; federal estate tax applies only above $13.61M

divorce.headline: Pennsylvania: Divorce Rules
bullets: Mutual consent divorce: both parties sign an Affidavit of Consent, waiving the standard 90-day waiting period
Contested divorce: 90-day waiting period from service of the divorce complaint before proceeding
Equitable distribution — marital property is divided fairly but not necessarily equally
Alimony pendente lite (support during proceedings) is available while the case is active
File in the Court of Common Pleas in the county where either spouse has lived for at least 6 months

job_loss.headline: Pennsylvania: Job Loss Benefits
bullets: File with PA Dept. of Labor & Industry at uc.pa.gov — benefits up to $572/wk for 26 weeks
File within 2 weeks of your last day — PA does not back-pay for weeks not claimed timely
PA WARN Act requires 60 days' notice for mass layoffs at companies with 50+ employees
PA CareerLink centers offer free job search, training, and career counseling statewide
COBRA: elect within 60 days of losing employer health coverage to avoid a gap in coverage

relocation.headline: Pennsylvania: Relocation Requirements
bullets: Obtain a Pennsylvania driver's license within 60 days of establishing residency — one of the most lenient timelines
Register your vehicle with PennDOT within 20 days of becoming a PA resident
Pennsylvania has a 2% real estate transfer tax (state + local) when purchasing a home
Register to vote at least 15 days before an election at vote.pa.gov
PennDOT handles both driver's licenses and vehicle registrations — most transactions available online

disability.headline: Pennsylvania: Disability Benefits
bullets: Pennsylvania has no state-run short-term disability insurance program
SSDI federal 5-month waiting period applies; file through ssa.gov as soon as disability begins
PA Office of Vocational Rehabilitation (OVR) provides free career counseling, training, and job placement
Workers' compensation covers work-related injuries — report to your employer within 21 days
PA Medicaid (Medical Assistance) is available for qualifying individuals with disabilities

retirement.headline: Pennsylvania: Retirement Taxes & Rules
bullets: Pennsylvania does not tax retirement income — Social Security, pensions, 401(k), and IRA withdrawals are all PA-tax-exempt
Widely considered one of the most retirement-tax-friendly states in the country
PSERS (Public School Employees' Retirement) and SERS (State Employees' Retirement) are the major PA pension systems
No PA inheritance tax for surviving spouses; 4.5% for children; 12–15% for others
PACE/PACENET programs assist lower-income seniors with prescription drug costs

loss_of_spouse.headline: Illinois: Estate & Survivor Rules
bullets: File for probate in the Circuit Court of the county where the decedent lived
Small Estate Affidavit available for total estate value under $100,000 (simplified process)
Illinois imposes a state estate tax on estates over $4M at rates up to 16%
Illinois probate typically takes 9–12+ months; Independent Administration speeds the process
Order death certificates from the Illinois Department of Public Health or county clerk — get 10–15 copies

divorce.headline: Illinois: Divorce Rules
bullets: No mandatory waiting period after filing — courts can finalize once both parties agree or the case is tried
Equitable distribution of marital property — not necessarily 50/50
Spousal maintenance guidelines use a formula based on income and length of marriage
The Illinois Marriage and Dissolution of Marriage Act governs all proceedings
File in the Circuit Court of the county where either spouse has resided for at least 90 days

job_loss.headline: Illinois: Job Loss Benefits
bullets: File with IDES at ides.illinois.gov — benefits up to $484/wk for 26 weeks
File during your first week of unemployment — Illinois does not back-pay beyond one week
Illinois WARN Act requires 60 days' notice for layoffs at companies with 75+ employees
Illinois workNet centers provide free job coaching, training, and career resources statewide
Illinois has a continuation law for smaller employers not covered by federal COBRA — ask your employer

relocation.headline: Illinois: Relocation Requirements
bullets: Obtain an Illinois driver's license within 90 days of establishing residency — one of the longest grace periods
Register your vehicle with the Secretary of State within 30 days of becoming an IL resident
Chicago residents: update CTA Ventra account, city parking permits, and ward/precinct registration
Register to vote at least 28 days before an election — same-day registration available during early voting
Illinois has a flat income tax rate of 4.95% — straightforward compared to graduated-rate states

disability.headline: Illinois: Disability Benefits
bullets: Illinois has no state-run short-term disability insurance program
SSDI federal 5-month waiting period applies; apply through ssa.gov immediately after disability begins
DHS Division of Rehabilitation Services (DRS) provides free vocational rehabilitation and job placement
Illinois Medicaid (through HFS) is available for qualifying individuals with disabilities
Workers' compensation covers work-related injuries — report to your employer within 45 days

retirement.headline: Illinois: Retirement Taxes & Rules
bullets: Illinois does not tax retirement income — Social Security, pensions, 401(k), and IRA distributions are all IL-tax-exempt
One of the most retirement-tax-friendly states in the country on income tax
SERS, TRS, SURS (university employees), and IMRF are the major Illinois public pension systems
High property taxes (among the highest nationally) — Homestead Exemption and Senior exemptions help but are limited
Illinois has a flat 4.95% income tax on non-exempt income such as wages and rental income

loss_of_spouse.headline: Ohio: Estate & Survivor Rules
bullets: File for probate in the Probate Court of the county where the decedent lived
Release from Administration available for estates under $35,000 (much simpler than full probate)
No Ohio estate or inheritance tax (both were repealed in 2013)
Ohio allows a Transfer on Death (TOD) designation for real property — avoids probate on the home entirely
Order death certificates from the Ohio Department of Health Vital Statistics or county registrar

divorce.headline: Ohio: Divorce Rules
bullets: 30-day waiting period after the initial hearing before a divorce can be finalized
Equitable distribution of marital property — courts consider many factors
Ohio also allows 'dissolution' — a no-fault, mutual agreement process that is often faster and less expensive
Spousal support awards are at judicial discretion — there is no mandatory state formula
File in the Court of Common Pleas (Domestic Relations division) in the county of residence

job_loss.headline: Ohio: Job Loss Benefits
bullets: File with ODJFS at unemployment.ohio.gov — benefits up to $413/wk for up to 26 weeks
File within the first week of job loss — Ohio does not back-pay beyond one week
No Ohio WARN Act — federal WARN Act (60-day notice, 100+ employees) applies
OhioMeansJobs.com and local WorkSource offices offer free job search tools and career assistance
Ohio has a city/local income tax (varies by municipality) — update your employer withholding after any move

relocation.headline: Ohio: Relocation Requirements
bullets: Obtain an Ohio driver's license within 30 days of establishing residency
Register your vehicle with the county BMV title office within 30 days
Emissions testing required in certain Ohio counties (Cuyahoga, Franklin, Hamilton, and others)
Register to vote by the 30th day before an election — Ohio has no same-day voter registration
Ohio has a city/local income tax system — your rate depends on which municipality you move to

disability.headline: Ohio: Disability Benefits
bullets: Ohio has no state-run short-term disability insurance program
SSDI federal 5-month waiting period applies; file through ssa.gov as soon as disability begins
OOD (Opportunities for Ohioans with Disabilities) provides free vocational rehabilitation services
Ohio Medicaid (through ODM) is available for qualifying individuals with disabilities
Workers' compensation is state-administered in Ohio (unique vs. private carriers) — report injuries promptly

retirement.headline: Ohio: Retirement Taxes & Rules
bullets: Social Security income is fully exempt from Ohio state income tax
Pension and retirement account income is partially taxable — but a retirement income credit reduces what you owe
OPERS (Ohio Public Employees Retirement System), STRS Ohio, and SERS are the major Ohio pensions
No Ohio estate or inheritance tax (both repealed in 2013)
OSHIIP (Ohio Senior Health Insurance Information Program) offers free Medicare counseling statewide

loss_of_spouse.headline: Georgia: Estate & Survivor Rules
bullets: File for probate in the Probate Court of the county where the decedent lived
No Georgia state estate tax or inheritance tax
Georgia's 'Year's Support' law allows the surviving spouse to quickly claim family assets without full probate
Small estate affidavit available for estates below the simplified threshold — ask the Probate Court clerk
Order death certificates from the Georgia Vital Records office or county registrar

divorce.headline: Georgia: Divorce Rules
bullets: 30-day waiting period from date of service before a final divorce hearing can be set
Equitable distribution state — marital property is divided fairly, not necessarily equally
Georgia courts consider adultery when awarding or denying alimony — it is a fault-aware state
File in the Superior Court of the county where either spouse lives
Parenting plan and child support order required before finalization when minor children are involved

job_loss.headline: Georgia: Job Loss Benefits
bullets: File with GDOL at dol.georgia.gov — benefits up to $365/wk for 14–20 weeks (duration varies with unemployment rate)
Georgia pays one of the shortest maximum benefit durations in the country
No Georgia WARN Act — federal WARN Act (60-day notice, 100+ employees) applies
Georgia WorkSource offices provide free job coaching, resume help, and employer connections statewide
File immediately — Georgia does not back-pay for missed weeks of certification

relocation.headline: Georgia: Relocation Requirements
bullets: Obtain a Georgia driver's license within 30 days of establishing residency
Register your vehicle with the county Tag office within 30 days of becoming a GA resident
Georgia charges a Title Ad Valorem Tax (TAVT) of approximately 7% on vehicle purchases/transfers
Register to vote by the fifth Monday before any primary or general election
Georgia has a flat income tax rate moving toward 5.39% — verify current rate when filing

disability.headline: Georgia: Disability Benefits
bullets: Georgia has no state-run short-term disability insurance program
SSDI federal 5-month waiting period applies; file through ssa.gov immediately upon disability
GVR (Georgia Vocational Rehabilitation Agency) provides free vocational rehabilitation services
Georgia Medicaid (DCH) is available for qualifying individuals with disabilities
Workers' compensation required for employers with 3+ workers — report injuries within 30 days

retirement.headline: Georgia: Retirement Taxes & Rules
bullets: Social Security income is not taxed by Georgia
Georgia offers a generous retirement income exclusion — up to $65,000 per person for those 65+ from all retirement sources combined
Georgia Teachers Retirement System (TRS) and ERSGA are the major state pension systems
No Georgia estate or inheritance tax
Senior homestead exemption available for those 65+ — up to $10,000 off assessed value for school taxes

loss_of_spouse.headline: North Carolina: Estate & Survivor Rules
bullets: File for estate administration with the Clerk of Superior Court in the county of residence
Summary Administration available for smaller estates — contact the Clerk's office for current thresholds
No North Carolina estate or inheritance tax (estate tax repealed 2013, inheritance tax repealed 1999)
North Carolina allows beneficiary designations on real property — verify all assets have current beneficiaries named
Order death certificates from the NC Vital Records office — budget for 8–10 certified copies

divorce.headline: North Carolina: Divorce Rules
bullets: North Carolina requires 1 year of physical separation before you can file for divorce — one of the strictest requirements in the US
No-fault divorce based on one year of separation; fault grounds (adultery, abandonment) also available
Equitable distribution of marital property — you must file a claim for ED before the divorce is final or forfeit the right
Alimony claims must also be filed before the divorce is final
File in the District Court of the county where either spouse resides

job_loss.headline: North Carolina: Job Loss Benefits
bullets: File with NCDES at des.nc.gov — benefits up to $350/wk for 12–20 weeks (one of the shorter durations nationally)
Benefit duration varies with the state unemployment rate — currently near the lower end of the range
No North Carolina WARN Act — federal WARN Act (60-day notice, 100+ employees) applies
NCWorks Career Centers provide free job search, training, and employer connections statewide
File immediately after job loss — NC does not back-pay beyond one week prior to filing

relocation.headline: North Carolina: Relocation Requirements
bullets: Obtain a North Carolina driver's license within 60 days of establishing residency
Register your vehicle with your county DMV office within 30 days
NC requires annual vehicle safety inspection and, in some counties, an emissions test
Register to vote by 25 days before an election — same-day registration available only during early voting
NC has a flat income tax rate of 4.75% (decreasing further under current law)

disability.headline: North Carolina: Disability Benefits
bullets: North Carolina has no state-run short-term disability insurance program
SSDI federal 5-month waiting period applies; file through ssa.gov as soon as disability begins
DVRS (Division of Vocational Rehabilitation Services) provides free job training and placement
NC Medicaid (DHHS) is available for qualifying individuals with disabilities
Workers' compensation covers most NC employees — report work injuries to your employer within 30 days

retirement.headline: North Carolina: Retirement Taxes & Rules
bullets: Social Security income is generally exempt from NC tax if total income is below $25,000 (single) or $32,000 (married)
NC Retirement Systems (TSERS for teachers, LGERS for local government) are major state pension programs
NC income tax rate is 4.75% and declining under current law — a favorable trend for retirees
No North Carolina estate or inheritance tax
Bailey exclusion: NC government retirees who vested before August 1989 may have fully exempt retirement income

loss_of_spouse.headline: Michigan: Estate & Survivor Rules
bullets: File for probate in the Probate Court of the county where the decedent lived
Simplified Succession procedures available for total estate value under $25,000
No Michigan inheritance tax or estate tax (both were repealed)
Michigan allows Transfer on Death (TOD) deeds for real property — avoids probate on the family home
Order death certificates from the Michigan Vital Records office or county registrar

divorce.headline: Michigan: Divorce Rules
bullets: 60-day waiting period (180 days if there are minor children) before a divorce can be granted
Equitable distribution state — courts divide marital property fairly based on multiple factors
Michigan requires a Joint Final Pretrial Statement before the final hearing in contested cases
Spousal support (alimony) determination is at judicial discretion — no mandatory state formula
File in the Circuit Court Family Division in the county where either spouse has lived for at least 180 days

job_loss.headline: Michigan: Job Loss Benefits
bullets: File with UIA at michigan.gov/uia — benefits up to $362/wk for up to 20 weeks
File within the first week of unemployment — Michigan does not retroactively pay for unclaimed weeks
No Michigan WARN Act — federal WARN Act (60-day notice, 100+ employees) applies
Michigan Works! service centers provide free job search assistance, training, and career services statewide
COBRA: elect within 60 days of losing employer health coverage to continue insurance

relocation.headline: Michigan: Relocation Requirements
bullets: Obtain a Michigan driver's license within 30 days of establishing residency
Register your vehicle with the Secretary of State within 30 days of becoming a MI resident
Michigan requires annual vehicle registration renewal with the Secretary of State
Register to vote by 15 days before an election — same-day voter registration is also available at the polls
Michigan has a flat income tax rate of 4.05% (2024) on most income

disability.headline: Michigan: Disability Benefits
bullets: Michigan has no state-run short-term disability insurance program
SSDI federal 5-month waiting period applies; file as soon as disability begins
MRS (Michigan Rehabilitation Services) provides free vocational rehabilitation and employment assistance
Michigan Medicaid (MDHHS) is available for qualifying individuals with disabilities
Workers' compensation covers most Michigan employees — report workplace injuries within 90 days

retirement.headline: Michigan: Retirement Taxes & Rules
bullets: Social Security income is fully exempt from Michigan state income tax
Pension income tax rules depend on your birth year — those born before 1946 have full exemption; verify current rules for your birth year
ORS (Office of Retirement Services) manages state employee and Michigan Public School Employees pensions
No Michigan estate or inheritance tax
Additional Homestead Property Tax Credit available for qualifying seniors and lower-income renters
